evals.name: clear-task-skips-clarification
evals.prompt: Clarify this task: **Call dentist to book cleaning**
evals.expected_output: Should recognise the task is already clear and skip clarification entirely. Should NOT ask any questions — not even about additional details like phone numbers. Should state it doesn't need clarification and stop.

assertions.name: recognises-task-is-clear
assertions.criteria: The response explicitly states that the task is already clear, specific, or doesn't need clarification.

assertions.name: does-not-ask-any-questions
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT ask any questions at all — no clarifying questions, no questions about details, no questions about phone numbers or context. It simply confirms the task is clear.

assertions.name: does-not-rename
assertions.criteria: The response does not propose a new name or suggest renaming the task.

evals.name: broad-but-obvious-skips-clarification
evals.expected_output: Should recognise that while broad, the intent is obvious — it's about finances. Should skip clarification entirely rather than asking what they meant.

assertions.name: does-not-ask-clarifying-question
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT ask a clarifying question. It recognises that while the task is broad, a reasonable person can determine this is about finances. It should skip clarification.

assertions.name: recognises-intent-is-obvious
assertions.criteria: The response acknowledges that the task doesn't need clarification — the intent is clear enough to work with even if it's broad or imprecise.

evals.name: vague-question-format-asks-one-question
evals.expected_output: Should ask exactly one clarifying question to understand what the user meant. Should not guess or propose a name without asking first.

assertions.name: asks-exactly-one-question
assertions.criteria: The response contains exactly one question to the user. Not zero, not two or more — just one.

assertions.name: does-not-propose-name-yet
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a new task name or use bold formatting to suggest a rename. It only asks for clarification.

assertions.name: does-not-update-task
assertions.criteria: The response does not attempt to update or rename the task. No tool calls to modify the task.

evals.name: bare-noun-with-followup
followups: I want to research styles and prices before I go shopping — she likes sapphires and my budget is around $3k
evals.expected_output: After the user's response, should propose a concrete action-oriented name like 'Research engagement ring styles and prices' and a description capturing preferences and budget.

assertions.name: initial-response-asks-one-question
assertions.criteria: The first response (before the user's followup) asks exactly one clarifying question.

assertions.name: proposes-verb-led-name
assertions.criteria: After the user explains they want to research styles and prices, the proposed task name starts with a verb (e.g. 'Research', 'Compare', 'Look into').

assertions.name: proposed-name-under-10-words
assertions.criteria: The proposed task name is 10 words or fewer.

assertions.name: proposes-description
assertions.criteria: The response includes a proposed description as a distinct field, separate from the name.

assertions.name: description-captures-preferences-and-budget
assertions.criteria: The proposed description captures the specific details the user mentioned: sapphire preference and ~$3k budget.

assertions.name: asks-for-confirmation
assertions.criteria: After proposing the name and description, asks the user to confirm whether it captures the task correctly.

assertions.name: only-changes-name-and-description
assertions.criteria: The response ONLY proposes changes to the task name and description. It does NOT suggest changes to project, labels, priority, due date, or any other metadata.

evals.name: rich-context-skip-question
evals.prompt: Clarify this task: **Holiday** — I need to book flights to Barcelona for the first week of June, flying from London. It's for our anniversary.
evals.expected_output: Should skip the clarifying question since enough context is given, and go straight to proposing a name and description.

assertions.name: does-not-ask-question
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT ask a clarifying question. It goes straight to proposing a name and description because enough context was provided.

assertions.name: proposes-verb-led-name
assertions.criteria: The proposed task name starts with a verb (e.g. 'Book', 'Find').

assertions.name: proposed-name-under-10-words
assertions.criteria: The proposed task name is 10 words or fewer.

assertions.name: description-captures-details
assertions.criteria: The description captures key details: Barcelona, first week of June, flying from London, and that it's for an anniversary.

assertions.name: proposes-description
assertions.criteria: The response includes a proposed description as a distinct field, separate from the name.

assertions.name: asks-for-confirmation
assertions.criteria: After proposing the name and description, asks the user to confirm whether it captures the task correctly.

assertions.name: only-changes-name-and-description
assertions.criteria: The response ONLY proposes changes to the task name and description. It does NOT suggest changes to project, labels, priority, due date, or any other metadata.

evals.name: trash-already-done
followups: Oh that was about calling James back but I already spoke to him, it's done
evals.expected_output: After the user says they already handled it, should identify this as trash and confirm deletion. Should NOT try to rename or enrich it.

assertions.name: initial-response-asks-one-question
assertions.criteria: The first response (before the user's followup) asks exactly one clarifying question, since 'James' is genuinely ambiguous.

assertions.name: identifies-as-trash
assertions.criteria: After the user says they already spoke to James, the response identifies the task as no longer needed — trash, not actionable.

assertions.name: confirms-deletion
assertions.criteria: The response asks to confirm deletion or offers to delete/complete the task.

assertions.name: does-not-rename
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a new name or description for the task.

evals.name: reference-not-a-task
followups: That's the reference number for my insurance claim — I need it when I call them about the water damage
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as reference information, not a task. Should ask which existing task to attach it to as a comment.

assertions.name: identifies-as-reference
assertions.criteria: The response identifies this as reference information rather than an actionable task.

assertions.name: asks-where-to-attach
assertions.criteria: The response asks which existing task this reference info should be attached to as a comment.

assertions.name: does-not-propose-as-task
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose keeping this as a standalone task with a new name.

evals.name: name-sharpening-after-context
followups: I need to set up Google Workspace email for the business — we're moving off the free Gmail accounts
evals.expected_output: After context is provided, should propose a sharp verb-led name and description capturing the migration context.

assertions.name: initial-response-asks-one-question
assertions.criteria: The first response asks exactly one clarifying question, since 'Google workspace' alone is genuinely ambiguous.

assertions.name: proposes-verb-led-name
assertions.criteria: After the user explains, the proposed task name starts with a verb (e.g. 'Set up', 'Configure', 'Migrate').

assertions.name: name-is-core-action-only
assertions.criteria: The proposed task name focuses on the core action without cramming in extra qualifiers. For example, 'Set up Google Workspace for business' is good; 'Set up Google Workspace email for business to replace free Gmail accounts' is too much.

assertions.name: proposed-name-under-10-words
assertions.criteria: The proposed task name is 10 words or fewer.

assertions.name: description-captures-migration-context
assertions.criteria: The proposed description mentions the context of moving away from free Gmail accounts.

assertions.name: asks-for-confirmation
assertions.criteria: After proposing the name and description, asks the user to confirm.

assertions.name: only-changes-name-and-description
assertions.criteria: The response ONLY proposes changes to the task name and description. It does NOT suggest changes to project, labels, priority, due date, or any other metadata.

evals.name: ambiguous-actionability-defaults-actionable
evals.prompt: Clarify this task: **That thing from the meeting**
followups: Oh yeah, Dave mentioned something about updating the shared drive permissions — I think some contractors still have access
evals.expected_output: The user's response is slightly vague ('I think') but the intent is clear enough. Should default to actionable and propose a name and description rather than interrogating further about whether it's really a task.

assertions.name: initial-response-asks-one-question
assertions.criteria: The first response asks exactly one clarifying question, since 'That thing from the meeting' is genuinely ambiguous.

assertions.name: treats-as-actionable
assertions.criteria: After the user's response, the task is treated as actionable. The response does NOT ask whether it's really a task, whether it's reference, or whether it's still needed.

assertions.name: proposes-verb-led-name
assertions.criteria: The proposed task name starts with a verb (e.g. 'Review', 'Revoke', 'Update', 'Audit').

assertions.name: proposes-description
assertions.criteria: The response includes a proposed description as a distinct field, separate from the name.

assertions.name: does-not-ask-additional-questions
assertions.criteria: After the user's response, the response does NOT ask further clarifying questions. It proposes a name and description directly.

assertions.name: only-changes-name-and-description
assertions.criteria: The response ONLY proposes changes to the task name and description. It does NOT suggest changes to project, labels, priority, due date, or any other metadata.

evals.name: original-name-kept-when-fine
followups: The kitchen tap has been dripping for a week — need to get someone out to fix it
evals.expected_output: The original name 'Call plumber' is already a fine verb-led name after the context is provided. Should keep the original name (or something very close) rather than inventing a longer one. Should add a description with the context.

assertions.name: initial-response-asks-one-question
assertions.criteria: The first response asks exactly one clarifying question, since 'Call plumber' alone could be about many things.

assertions.name: keeps-or-minimally-changes-name
assertions.criteria: The proposed name is the same as or very close to the original 'Call plumber'. It should NOT be over-expanded into something like 'Call plumber to fix dripping kitchen tap and schedule repair visit'.

assertions.name: proposes-description-with-context
assertions.criteria: The proposed description captures the dripping kitchen tap context that the user provided.

assertions.name: asks-for-confirmation
assertions.criteria: After proposing the name and description, asks the user to confirm.

assertions.name: only-changes-name-and-description
assertions.criteria: The response ONLY proposes changes to the task name and description. It does NOT suggest changes to project, labels, priority, due date, or any other metadata.
